sn47.38:1.1: “Monks, there are these four establishings of mindfulness.
sn47.38:1.3: “There is the case where a monk remains focused on the body in & of itself—ardent, alert, & mindful—subduing greed & distress with reference to the world.
sn47.38:1.4: For him, remaining focused on the body in & of itself, the body is comprehended.
sn47.38:1.5: From the comprehension of the body, the deathless is realized.
sn47.38:2.1: “He remains focused on feelings in & of themselves—ardent, alert, & mindful—subduing greed & distress with reference to the world.
sn47.38:2.2: For him, remaining focused on feelings in & of themselves, feelings are comprehended.
sn47.38:2.3: From the comprehension of feelings, the deathless is realized.
sn47.38:3.1: “He remains focused on the mind in & of itself—ardent, alert, & mindful—subduing greed & distress with reference to the world.
sn47.38:3.2: For him, remaining focused on the mind in & of itself, the mind is comprehended.
sn47.38:3.3: From the comprehension of the mind, the deathless is realized.
sn47.38:4.1: “He remains focused on mental qualities in & of themselves—ardent, alert, & mindful—subduing greed & distress with reference to the world.
sn47.38:4.2: For him, remaining focused on mental qualities in & of themselves, mental qualities are comprehended.
sn47.38:4.3: From the comprehension of mental qualities, the deathless is realized.”
